content_of_post_to_comment: I am working from home at my job as a software engineer this morning. My mainsplaining colleague is on sick leave, my uncle has left me alone since I told him not to tell me to see my abusive dad, the heat has made way to rain, I haven’t been harrassed on public transport for a while, and I don’t need to go to an office and deal with micro-aggressions. Oh, and I have stopped dating. Peace has been re-enstated. It has required literally hiding and not making eye contact with anyone on the ubahn, but hey, I am happy.
comment: Strikes me that the 'peace' here is tightly coupled to reduced visibility—no eye contact, no exposure, no dating. When safety relies on disappearance, it raises the question of what kind of public life is still possible.
